key_risks: Sticky 4.2% CPI/4.1% PCE could push July FOMC hawkish, hitting rate-sensitive banks and biotech
Semiconductor momentum reversal if Micron-driven enthusiasm fades or QQQ weakness persists
Biotech high volatility (34% vol) and reversal risk after sharp 30d run
Small-cap/cyclical rotation could unwind if growth data softens
Concentration in US cyclicals underperforms if defensive bond proxies lead

portfolio.rationale: Strong recent breakout with 13.8% 30d gain at 52w high; XBI momentum supported by risk-on small/mid cap rotation and improving claims data.
Flash manufacturing PMI 55.7 (49-month high) supports cyclicals; XLI at 52w highs with broad participation.
Defensive sector breaking out (+5.25% 30d, +7.17% vs SPY) with low beta amid sticky inflation prints; offers ballast.
Micron blowout guide ($50B revenue, 86% GM) signals HBM/AI demand re-acceleration; sector catalyst for SMH near-term.
KRE at 52w high with steepening curve (10y-2y +30bps), low claims, and improving credit; benefits from small-cap rotation.

portfolio_rationale: Tilts to cyclical breadth (industrials, regional banks) and idiosyncratic catalysts (Micron-driven semis, biotech breakout) while healthcare provides lower-beta ballast against sticky CPI/PCE prints ahead of June CPI and FOMC.
rationale_summary: Balance pro-cyclical breakout sectors with a defensive healthcare anchor and a semiconductor catalyst play following Micron's guidance.
